role_title: Senior Product Manager, Enterprise Picking & Tools

location.office_time_required: Flex First — no fixed office requirement; regular in-person events

secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with enterprise/B2B operations flavor

level_signals: Owns vision, strategy, and execution for an enterprise product area
Expected to align senior stakeholders and represent roadmap externally with retail partners

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of product management experience, ideally within enterprise B2B or operationally complex environments
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within enterprise B2B or operationally complex environments

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've built products end-to-end and know how to drive initiatives from early concept through scaled delivery.
You're highly data-literate and comfortable navigating ambiguity with analytical rigor, using data to prioritize ruthlessly and make sound tradeoffs.
You communicate complex ideas with clarity and influence — aligning cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders around shared goals without direct authority.
You understand the shopper and retailer experience deeply, and you hold yourself accountable to meaningful customer and commercial outcomes.
You operate with an entrepreneurial mindset, comfortable moving fast in evolving environments while maintaining high standards.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in B2B product management, enterprise software, client services, or partner success teams
Experience building products for frontline or operational users (e.g., retail associates, delivery workers, warehouse staff)

explicitly_excluded: Only hiring in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Nova Scotia (Canada); US candidates not mentioned as eligible

scope.surface: Enterprise Picking & Tools — in-store picking workflows, shopper-facing order management tools, and retailer configuration/monitoring tooling across pick & deliver, pickup, and delivery-only fulfillment models
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / marketplace platform; retail partner enablement
core_responsibilities: Own enterprise picking experiences — from how shoppers receive and action orders in-store, to the tools retailers use to configure, monitor, and optimize their fulfillment operations
Build deep understanding of shopper workflows and retailer operational pain points to design tools that improve picking accuracy, speed, and shopper satisfaction
Work closely with retail partners to ensure our tooling integrates naturally into their in-store environments
Partner with enterprise sales and retailer success teams to support partner onboarding, drive adoption of new features, and contribute to retailer NPS and retention
Define platform solutions that scale across retailer sizes, store formats, and fulfillment models — including pick & deliver, pickup, and delivery-only
Represent the picking and tools roadmap with internal stakeholders and, where relevant, with external retail partners

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Equity mentioned (new hire + annual refresh grants)
Clear product ownership scope — enterprise picking and retailer tools
Flex First remote policy with no mandatory office percentage
Cross-functional team (engineers, designers, data scientists) dedicated to this PM
red_flags: Canada-only hiring (Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Nova Scotia) — eliminates US and international candidates
Compensation stated in CAD only — no USD equivalent provided
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
4+ years minimum is relatively low for a 'Senior' title, suggesting possible title inflation or broad candidate targeting

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior PM' and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and retailer partner context)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from explicit Canada-only hiring statement)
compensation.base_range_cad (custom field — schema base_range_usd not applicable; CAD range stated)
ambiguities: Role described as 'remote' under Flex First policy but restricted to specific Canadian provinces — not truly globally remote; treated as remote-global with geographic restriction
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in the JD
The 'ideally within enterprise B2B' qualifier on the 4+ years requirement is ambiguous — treated as hard on years, with B2B context as a qualifier rather than a separate soft requirement
